raw.title: Gemma 4 12B: Google's Unified Multimodal Model runs on 16GB laptops
raw.body: Google released Gemma 4 12B, an encoder-free multimodal model that replaces dedicated vision encoders with a lighter projection path. Positioned as 'agentic AI' capable of running on laptops with 16GB of memory. HN discussion: 1,045 points, 388 comments with mixed reactions about whether encoder-free architecture is meaningful and how well it performs in real-world local use.

raw.title: Ted Chiang: Artificial Intelligence Is Not Conscious (771 points, 1,347 comments)
raw.body: Acclaimed sci-fi author Ted Chiang argued in The Atlantic against AI consciousness claims. The essay sparked one of the most-commented HN threads of June 2026, with deep philosophical debate about the nature of consciousness and whether current AI architectures could ever achieve it.

raw.title: Uber caps employee AI coding tools at $1,500/month (612 points)
raw.body: Simon Willison analyzed Uber's decision to cap employee AI coding tools at $1,500/month as a signal for enterprise AI pricing economics. HN community split between seeing it as disciplined cost control versus evidence that AI economics are still uncertain. The discussion raised broader questions about the ROI of enterprise AI tools.

raw.title: Failing grades soar with AI usage at UC Berkeley CS programs
raw.body: A report showed surging failing grades in UC Berkeley CS classes in spring 2026, with professors pointing to heavy AI reliance and weaker math preparation. The HN discussion debated whether AI is the primary driver or amplifying pre-existing problems in CS education.

raw.title: AI Agent Discovers 21 Zero-Day Vulnerabilities in Open-Source Projects
raw.body: An AI-powered security system autonomously discovered 21 previously unknown zero-day vulnerabilities in popular open-source projects. The findings have significant implications for both cybersecurity and the broader debate about AI safety. Anthropic also detailed sandboxing approaches for Claude, though HN was skeptical about real containment.

raw.title: Anthropic claims AI writes large share of its own code, could help build successor
raw.body: Anthropic disclosed that AI already writes a substantial portion of its own code and suggested it could help build its successor model. This recursive self-improvement claim sparked intense debate about the trajectory of AI development. Meanwhile, Google employees internally shared memes mocking AI coding tools despite leadership claims that 75% of new code is AI-generated.
